en.text: loss for him, he is not denied the chance of the fortune to see the Supersoul
by the mind (enchanted by sense objects)
by, under the guidance of a bona fide spiritual master, practice (of meditation on the Supreme Lord)
at the top, between the eyes (centering the concentration)
free from the defects of attachment, fear, and anger
gains the perfection of many lifetimes’ endeavours
and a heart that is never discouraged
for a person of uncontrolled mind
without renunciation of fruitive attachment (the basis of niṣkām karma-yoga)
by a person of uncontrolled mind
powerful uncontrollable by even the intelligence
renders service to Me by engaging in the devotional practices based on hearing and chanting My glories
worships Me with devotion (based on hearing, chanting, and remembering)
and situated in the spiritual nature
on the path of attaining the Absolute
of Kuśa grass, deerskin, and cloth
controlled by the yogic practice of dhāraṇā
state within which all association with unhappiness is severed
to the objects of the senses – sound, touch, form, taste, and smell
yogī who has conquered the mind
to the jñānī, the person of knowledge
to the karmī, person of action
situated in the one unchanging spiritual nature
and by the mind (with the vision of the futility of mundane enjoyment)
completely steadying the mind within the self
to Me – he never fails to be conscious of Me
and does not become attached to action in pursuit of their enjoyment
must not be made to fall down into the bondage of the material world
nor is a yogī one who merely renounces physical activities
A sannyāsī is not one who merely renounces the fire sacrifice (agnihotra yajña) and other prescribed duties
of emancipation by the destruction of material existence
of mind withdrawn from hankering for material enjoyment
liberation in the form of both self-realisation and Supersoul-realisation
turbulent disturbing the intelligence, body, and senses
of the peaceful (without attraction and aversion)
and of equal vision for earth, stone, and gold
a sannyāsī, one who has given up all for the Lord
situated in the heart of every being (as the Supersoul, My four-armed form measuring one prādeś, the distance between the thumb and forefinger)
and renounces all thoughts of enjoyment
in all circumstances (whether engaged in the execution of scripturally prescribed duties or not)
and is a seer of the same spirituality everywhere
Of all types of yogīs on the paths of karma, jñān, tapasyā, aṣṭāṅga-yoga, bhakti, etc
for spiritual success to attain the darśan or transcendental vision of the Supersoul
his consciousness cleansed of all worldly attachment
to the performer of severe austerities (such as Chāndrāyaṇ)
For that person accomplished in yoga, that is, when he has achieved meditation
loss for him, he is not denied the happiness of the heavenly plane
by his practice of the previous life
and deviated from the paths of both karma and yoga
he is said to have attained to yoga
must be delivered from the bondage of the material world
by the appropriate method of arduous practice (sādhana)
subjugating all mental and sensual activity
The yogī who is united with Brahma, the Absolute, who realises himself as qualitatively identical with Brahma
and whose mind deviates from yoga (due to a lack of practice and abnegation)
and he is a yogī, one in union with the Lord
whose effort in works is regulated
and whose sleeping and waking hours are in proper measure
For the person whose eating and recreation are regulated
engaging the mind in yogic meditation
for self-purification, to attain spiritual realisation
by the pure self of the same nature as the Supersoul
a person who engages in yoga practice due to his faith in yoga scriptures
with firm faith in the holy scriptures which corroborate the superexcellence of devotion for Me
given to pure and honest practices
who is free from the action of the mode of passion
In cold and heat, happiness and unhappiness

ru.text: полностью очистив сердце от всего мирского
обладающие духовной мудростью или пребывающие в медитации
сосредоточившись на осознании души и Высшей Души
